verse_context_groups.label: Boundary — reasoned disputation as inner-faculty engagement
verse_context_groups.context_description: Jude 9: Michael contending with the devil in disputation about the body of Moses. The dialegō here is the form taken by contested engagement between spiritual beings — reasoned disputation as the mode of serious inner-faculty engagement even in angelic conflict. P-status: borderline.

anchor_verse.verse_text: Jude 9 But when the archangel Michael , contending with the devil , was disputing about the body of Moses , he did not presume to pronounce a blasphemous judgment , but said , “The Lord rebuke you .”
anchor_verse.meaning: dialegō names the dispute/disputation as a form of inner-faculty engagement — even angelic conflict over a contested matter takes the form of reasoned disputation.
anchor_verse.anchor_rationale: Michael does not presume to pronounce judgment but engages in reasoned disputation (dialegō) — the form of serious inner-faculty engagement. Boundary case: the subject is angelic, and the phenomenon is at the edge of M15's scope.

verses.verse_text: Jude 9 But when the archangel Michael , contending with the devil , was disputing about the body of Moses , he did not presume to pronounce a blasphemous judgment , but said , “The Lord rebuke you .”
verses.meaning: dialegō names the dispute/disputation as a form of inner-faculty engagement — even angelic conflict over a contested matter takes the form of reasoned disputation.

verse_context_groups.label: Set aside or NR — no inner-being phenomenon / outside programme scope
verse_context_groups.context_description: Legal determination (diaginōskō, Acts); extended talking causing Eutychus to fall asleep (dialegō, borderline); name translation (methermēneuō); arbitrator role declined by Jesus (meristēs).

verses.verse_text: Act 23:15 Now therefore you , along with the council , give notice to the tribune to bring him down to you , as though you were going to determine his case more exactly . And we are ready to kill him before he comes near .”
verses.meaning: diaginōskō NR status — not recorded in programme as inner-being content. Legal determination function.

verses.verse_text: Act 24:22 But Felix , having a rather accurate knowledge of the Way , put them off , saying , “ When Lysias the tribune comes down , I will decide your case .”
verses.meaning: diaginōskō NR status — legal determination, not inner-being content.

verses.verse_text: Act 20:9 And a young man named Eutychus , sitting at the window , sank into a deep sleep as Paul talked still longer . And being overcome by sleep , he fell down from the third story and was taken up dead .
verses.meaning: dialegō names the extended talking that caused Eutychus to fall — the length of Paul's reasoning discourse.

verses.verse_text: Act 13:8 But Elymas the magician ( for that is the meaning of his name ) opposed them , seeking to turn the proconsul away from the faith .
verses.meaning: methermēneuō set aside as P-status — factual name translation without inner-being content in the span.

verses.verse_text: Luk 12:14 But he said to him , “ Man , who made me a judge or arbitrator over you ?”
verses.meaning: Names the role Jesus declines — the term identifies the capacity of one appointed to adjudicate between parties, which Jesus refuses to assume over the man's dispute.
